title: Establish liminal-server crate with binary entry point and error taxonomy
purpose: Bootstrap the liminal-server crate with Cargo.toml, a binary entry point at main.rs, a library root at lib.rs for testability, and the ServerError enum. This is the leaf dependency for every other server brief. The crate wraps the liminal library -- it adds deployment infrastructure (config, networking, clustering, health) but never messaging semantics. main.rs parses CLI arguments and delegates to lib.rs; lib.rs declares the module skeleton that SRV-002 through SRV-006 populate.
task: Create the liminal-server crate from scratch. Set up the Cargo.toml with dependencies on liminal, beamr, clap, toml, and thiserror. Create main.rs as a thin binary entry point that parses the --config CLI argument and calls into the library. Create lib.rs with module declarations for config, server, cluster, health, and error -- all as skeleton modules that later briefs fill in. Define ServerError covering configuration, listener, cluster, shutdown, and health failure cases, implementing std::error::Error and Debug via thiserror. Register the crate as a workspace member.

requirements.title: Create crate manifest with liminal dependency
requirements.spec: crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml SHALL declare the liminal-server crate with liminal as a dependency. The crate SHALL use the workspace edition, rust-version, and lint settings. The crate SHALL depend on liminal, beamr, clap (with derive feature), toml, thiserror, and tracing. The crate SHALL NOT depend on aion, haematite, or any library that duplicates liminal's messaging primitives.
acceptance: Cargo.toml exists at crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml with [package] name = "liminal-server"
liminal appears in [dependencies] as a path dependency
beamr, clap, toml, thiserror, and tracing appear in [dependencies]
Workspace lints inherited via [lints] workspace = true
No dependency on aion, haematite, or any ORM/database library

files: crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:1-14 — current manifest exists with package metadata, `liminal` path dependency, and workspace lints, but it is missing `rust-version.workspace = true` and all required non-liminal dependencies.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:1-16 — nearest manifest convention: workspace package fields, `beamr = { version = "0.6.4", features = ["json"] }`, `thiserror = "2"`, and `[lints] workspace = true`.
Cargo.toml:5-23 — workspace edition/rust-version/package metadata and deny lints that the server crate must inherit.
Cargo.lock:587-591 — current lock entry for `liminal-server` only records `liminal`; adding clap/toml/tracing/anyhow will update the lockfile during build/check.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-001.json:14-23 — R1 acceptance criteria and forbidden dependency list.
context: Treat the existing server manifest as an update, not a greenfield create: it already has `name = "liminal-server"`, workspace edition/license/repository/authors, `liminal = { path = "../liminal" }`, and workspace lints.
The existing `liminal` crate establishes dependency versions/style: use the same beamr version/features as `liminal` unless there is a deliberate reason not to.
R3 requires `anyhow` in `main.rs`; R1’s dependency list omits it, but the binary will not compile with `anyhow::Result` unless `anyhow` is added to this manifest too.

Do not add `aion`, `haematite`, ORM/database crates, or alternate messaging primitives. Even though later shutdown design mentions durable flushing, this bootstrap crate should keep durability/messaging delegated to `liminal`.
scout.approach: Update `crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml` to mirror the workspace package pattern: keep the existing package metadata, add `rust-version.workspace = true`, keep `[lints] workspace = true`, keep `liminal = { path = "../liminal" }`, and add `beamr`, `clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }`, `toml`, `thiserror`, `tracing`, plus `anyhow` for the R3 top-level binary result. Prefer the existing local versions where known (`beamr = { version = "0.6.4", features = ["json"] }`, `thiserror = "2"`) and avoid speculative dependencies such as `serde` until SRV-002 actually introduces config deserialization.
scout.notes: Because `Cargo.lock` is tracked and currently shows only `liminal` under `liminal-server`, the developer should expect a legitimate lockfile diff after adding dependencies and running Cargo. Also watch the workspace lints: `unwrap`, `expect`, and `panic` are denied, so dependency-driven examples from clap docs that use `.unwrap()` should not be copied into production code.

files_changed.note: Added workspace rust-version inheritance, required liminal/beamr/clap/toml/thiserror/tracing dependencies, anyhow for top-level binary errors, and preserved workspace lint inheritance.
Updated by Cargo to include the newly declared liminal-server dependency graph.

dev.how: The existing liminal-server manifest now declares the package under the workspace metadata pattern, keeps liminal as a path dependency, adds beamr with the same version/features as liminal, adds clap with derive, toml, thiserror, tracing, and anyhow for main.rs, and retains [lints] workspace = true. No forbidden direct dependencies such as aion, haematite, or ORM/database crates were added.

stories.note: cargo build -p liminal-server succeeds and produces the standalone liminal-server executable target.

requirements.title: Register crate as workspace member
requirements.spec: The workspace Cargo.toml SHALL include crates/liminal-server in its members list. The crate SHALL compile as part of the workspace build.
acceptance: Cargo.toml at workspace root lists "crates/liminal-server" in [workspace] members
cargo check --workspace includes liminal-server without error

files: Cargo.toml:1-3 — workspace currently declares `members = ["crates/liminal", "crates/liminal-server"]`, so the member-registration acceptance is already satisfied.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — root lint policy applies to workspace builds and will make `cargo check --workspace`/clippy sensitive to panics and unwraps.
crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:1-14 — the member crate already exists but must be completed before a meaningful workspace check after SRV-001 changes.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-001.json:33-39 — R2 acceptance requires root membership and successful workspace compilation.
context: No root manifest edit appears necessary right now: `crates/liminal-server` is already in the `[workspace] members` list.
The root manifest uses a compact one-line members array; preserve that style if touching it for any reason rather than reformatting unrelated workspace content.
R2’s build acceptance depends on the rest of SRV-001 compiling; failures are more likely to come from missing server modules/dependencies than from workspace registration.
The workspace lint settings deny `unwrap`, `expect`, and `panic`, so even placeholder code added for compileability must return `Result` or otherwise avoid panic-like stubs.
scout.approach: Leave `Cargo.toml` membership as-is unless the implementation branch has diverged. After finishing R1/R3/R4/R5, run `cargo check --workspace` to prove the server crate is included and compiles with the same lint/package settings as `liminal`; if Cargo rewrites only the lockfile due to new dependencies, treat that as part of the manifest dependency update rather than a root-membership change.
scout.notes: Do not add a second workspace member entry or create a separate workspace under `crates/liminal-server`; that would complicate the single-binary/package story and can hide the crate from the intended root workspace checks.

dev.how: The root Cargo.toml already listed "crates/liminal-server" in [workspace] members, so no root manifest edit was needed. cargo check --workspace includes and checks liminal-server successfully.

checklist.note: Workspace membership includes crates/liminal-server and the crate compiles as part of cargo check --workspace.

acceptance.criterion: Cargo.toml at workspace root lists "crates/liminal-server" in [workspace] members
acceptance.evidence: Cargo.toml:1-2 lists members = ["crates/liminal", "crates/liminal-server"].

acceptance.criterion: cargo check --workspace includes liminal-server without error
acceptance.evidence: Command `cargo check --workspace` exited 0 and reported `Finished dev profile`; root Cargo.toml:2 includes crates/liminal-server in the workspace membership being checked.

requirements.title: Create binary entry point with CLI argument parsing
requirements.spec: main.rs SHALL parse a --config <path> CLI argument via clap and call into the library crate to start the server. main.rs SHALL use anyhow for top-level error reporting. main.rs SHALL NOT contain business logic, configuration parsing, or server lifecycle code -- it delegates to lib.rs. IF --config is not provided, THEN main.rs SHALL exit with a usage error.
acceptance: main.rs exists at crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs
Running the binary with --help prints usage including --config
Running without --config exits with a non-zero code and a usage message
main.rs contains fewer than 30 lines of logic (excluding use statements and comments)
cargo build -p liminal-server produces a single executable binary

files: crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs:1-3 — current binary just prints `liminal-server`; replace with a small clap parser and library delegation.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-001.json:49-58 — R3 requires `--config <path>`, clap usage errors when absent, anyhow top-level reporting, and fewer than 30 lines of logic.
docs/design/server/stories.json:5-9 — S1 motivation: operator starts the server with a single config file without writing Rust.
docs/design/server/design.json:66-90 — intended server structure says `main.rs` is the binary entry point and `lib.rs`/`error.rs` hold reusable API pieces.
Cargo.toml:17-23 — clippy/rust lints deny unwrap/expect/panic in production entry-point code.
context: There are no existing clap patterns in this repo, so use standard clap derive directly: a private `Cli` struct with `#[derive(Debug, clap::Parser)]` and a required `#[arg(long)] config: PathBuf` field.
A non-optional `PathBuf` field with `#[arg(long)]` makes clap emit a usage error and non-zero exit when `--config` is omitted, satisfying the no-config acceptance without manual argument validation.
`main.rs` should return `anyhow::Result<()>` and use `?` to bridge the library’s `ServerError` once R5 implements `std::error::Error`.

There is a brief tension between “call into the library crate to start the server” and the SRV-001 boundaries forbidding real config/server lifecycle implementation; keep the binary thin and delegate to a minimal library API, not inline startup/config parsing in `main.rs`.
scout.approach: Replace `main.rs` with roughly: import `std::path::PathBuf` and `clap::Parser`, define a private `Cli { config: PathBuf }`, parse with `Cli::parse()`, then call a public library entry point such as `liminal_server::run(cli.config.as_path())?` or `liminal_server::server::run(...)`, returning `anyhow::Result<()>`. Keep all actual loading/listening behavior out of `main.rs`; if a compile-only run/start shim is needed for SRV-001, put it in a non-`mod.rs` library file and re-export it, then let SRV-002/SRV-003 replace the internals.
scout.notes: The file list for SRV-001 does not explicitly include a non-`mod.rs` server runtime file, but `main.rs` cannot both compile and delegate to the library without some exported symbol. Avoid putting that symbol’s implementation in `lib.rs` or `server/mod.rs` because CN3 forbids logic in module roots; use a tiny non-module-root file if necessary.

files_changed.note: Replaced placeholder println binary with clap Parser-based --config argument handling and anyhow::Result top-level error reporting.
Added the leaf library entry point that main.rs delegates to, keeping lifecycle/config logic out of main.rs and mod.rs files.
Added CN3-compliant declaration/re-export for the runtime leaf.
Re-exported the run entry point used by main.rs.

dev.how: main.rs defines a small private clap-derived Cli with required #[arg(long)] config: PathBuf, returns anyhow::Result<()>, parses arguments with Cli::parse(), and delegates startup to liminal_server::run(...)? without inline configuration parsing or lifecycle code. clap supplies the non-zero usage error when --config is omitted; --help shows --config.

checklist.note: Binary entry point compiles, cargo build -p liminal-server succeeds, --help includes --config, and running without --config exits with status 2 and usage output.

stories.note: The binary accepts a single --config <path> operator entry point while deferring actual config loading to later briefs.
The package builds the liminal-server executable as the deployment artifact.

acceptance.criterion: main.rs exists at crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs:1-17 exists and contains the binary entry point.

acceptance.criterion: Running the binary with --help prints usage including --config
acceptance.evidence: Manual CLI check `target/debug/liminal-server --help` printed `Usage: liminal-server --config <CONFIG>` and an Options entry for `--config <CONFIG>`.

acceptance.criterion: Running without --config exits with a non-zero code and a usage message
acceptance.evidence: Manual CLI check `target/debug/liminal-server` exited with code 2 and printed `error: the following required arguments were not provided: --config <CONFIG>` plus usage.

acceptance.criterion: main.rs contains fewer than 30 lines of logic (excluding use statements and comments)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs:5-17 contains the Cli struct and main function; the whole file is 17 lines.

requirements.title: Create library root with module declarations and re-exports
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare pub mod for config, server, cluster, health, and error. lib.rs SHALL re-export ServerError and the primary public types from each module. lib.rs SHALL contain ONLY module declarations and re-exports -- no logic (CN3). Placeholder mod.rs files SHALL exist for config, server, cluster, and health modules so that cargo check passes.
acceptance: lib.rs exists at crates/liminal-server/src/lib.rs
lib.rs declares pub mod config, server, cluster, health, error
lib.rs re-exports ServerError
lib.rs contains zero function definitions, zero trait definitions, zero struct definitions
Placeholder mod.rs files exist for config/, server/, cluster/, health/
cargo check -p liminal-server passes

files: docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-001.json:68-89 — R4 acceptance for `lib.rs` declarations/re-exports and placeholder module roots.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-12 — local library-root convention: `pub mod ...` declarations followed by `pub use ...` re-exports, with no functions/structs.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — representative `mod.rs` convention: only submodule declarations and `pub use`, matching CN3.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:1-7 — another module-root example showing multi-line public re-exports without logic.
docs/design/server/design.json:83-95 — planned server library structure and later config module ownership.
context: `crates/liminal-server/src/lib.rs` does not exist yet; adding it will cause Cargo to build both the library target and the existing binary target for the package.

CN3 is stricter than ordinary Rust style here: `lib.rs` and every `mod.rs` should contain only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, no functions, structs, traits, constants with logic, or placeholder implementations.

The required placeholder directories are `config/`, `server/`, `cluster/`, and `health/`; their `mod.rs` files can be empty at first or contain only future-safe re-export declarations once real submodules exist.

`lib.rs` must re-export `ServerError`; re-exporting primary public types from empty placeholder modules is only possible once those modules actually define or re-export types, so do not invent messaging/config types just to satisfy wording.
scout.approach: Create `src/lib.rs` with only `pub mod cluster; pub mod config; pub mod error; pub mod health; pub mod server;` plus `pub use error::ServerError;` and any legitimate public re-exports that exist after adding the minimal delegated entry point required by R3. Create `src/config/mod.rs`, `src/server/mod.rs`, `src/cluster/mod.rs`, and `src/health/mod.rs` as CN3-compliant module roots; if no real submodules exist yet, empty files are acceptable Rust modules and avoid prematurely implementing SRV-002 through SRV-006.
scout.notes: Do not put `run`, `load_config`, listener binding, health checks, or placeholder `todo!()` bodies in any `mod.rs`. If the R3 delegation shim is needed, make it a leaf file such as `server/runtime.rs` and have `server/mod.rs` only declare/re-export it.

files_changed.note: Added only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
Added only pub mod runtime and pub use runtime::run.
Added the non-mod.rs leaf needed for binary delegation.
Added error module declared and re-exported from lib.rs.

dev.how: The library root declares pub mod cluster, config, error, health, and server, and re-exports ServerError plus the legitimate public run entry point. All module roots contain only declarations/re-exports or are empty placeholders, while implementation lives in a leaf runtime file to preserve CN3. cargo check -p liminal-server passes.

checklist.note: The library skeleton and re-exports compile alongside the binary entry point.

requirements.title: Define ServerError enum with thiserror
requirements.spec: ServerError SHALL cover: ConfigLoad (file read or parse failure), ConfigValidation (semantic validation failure), ListenerBind (address binding failure), ListenerAccept (connection accept failure), ClusterJoin (seed node connection failure), ClusterSync (state propagation failure), ShutdownTimeout (drain did not complete in time), ShutdownFlush (durable state flush failure), HealthEndpoint (HTTP health server failure). Each variant SHALL carry a descriptive message or source error. ServerError SHALL implement std::error::Error via thiserror and Debug. ServerError SHALL NOT use a catch-all variant -- every failure mode has a named variant.
acceptance: ServerError is defined in crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs
All nine variants listed are present with appropriate fields
ServerError derives Debug and uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)]
ServerError is re-exported from lib.rs
No catch-all or Other variant exists

files: docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-001.json:93-103 — R5 names all nine required `ServerError` variants and forbids catch-all variants.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — local simple thiserror style: doc comment, `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]`, named variants carrying `message: String`.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:3-48 — richer local error-taxonomy example with stable named variants, per-variant `#[error(...)]`, and optional message fields; no catch-all variant.
docs/design/server/design.json:174-206 — CN2/CN4/CN6-CN8 constraints that shape the error taxonomy across config, listener, cluster, shutdown, and health.
docs/design/server/checklist.json:5-10 — C3 checklist ties `ServerError` specifically to `std::error::Error` and `Debug`.
context: Define `ServerError` in a new `crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs` and re-export it from `lib.rs`; this is the public error type future server modules will use.

All nine variants must be named exactly enough for future code/tests to match: `ConfigLoad`, `ConfigValidation`, `ListenerBind`, `ListenerAccept`, `ClusterJoin`, `ClusterSync`, `ShutdownTimeout`, `ShutdownFlush`, and `HealthEndpoint`.

The local `LiminalError` uses message-only fields for many variants, which is acceptable under R5’s “descriptive message or source error” language and avoids overfitting to dependencies that later briefs have not introduced yet.
Do not add `Other`, `Internal`, `Unexpected`, or catch-all variants; future failures should map to one of the explicit categories or add a new named requirement later.
scout.approach: Create `error.rs` with a documented `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] pub enum ServerError` and one `#[error(...)]` attribute per required variant. Prefer stable fields such as `{ message: String }` for semantic/cluster/health failures and source-carrying fields only where the concrete source type is already clear; for example, listener bind/accept can carry an address/message and `std::io::Error`, while config load may use a message/path plus a boxed source if you want to support both IO and TOML parse errors under the same variant. Keep constructors/helpers optional and minimal; the acceptance is the taxonomy, Debug, Error, re-export, and no catch-all.
scout.notes: Be careful with thiserror formatting: fields like `Vec<String>` do not implement `Display`, so either format them with `:?` or store an already-joined `message: String`. Also avoid `Box<dyn Error>` unless it includes `Send + Sync + 'static` if it will be converted through `anyhow` from `main.rs`.

files_changed.note: Defined ServerError with the required nine named variants and thiserror Display implementations.
Re-exported ServerError from the library root.

dev.how: ServerError is defined in src/error.rs with #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] and the exact named failure categories: ConfigLoad, ConfigValidation, ListenerBind, ListenerAccept, ClusterJoin, ClusterSync, ShutdownTimeout, ShutdownFlush, and HealthEndpoint. Each variant carries a descriptive message: String and has a specific #[error(...)] message; no catch-all variant was added.

checklist.note: ServerError implements Debug and std::error::Error through thiserror and is re-exported from lib.rs.

acceptance.criterion: ServerError is defined in crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:1-3 defines pub enum ServerError.

acceptance.criterion: All nine variants listed are present with appropriate fields
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:6,10,14,18,22,26,30,34,38 defines ConfigLoad, ConfigValidation, ListenerBind, ListenerAccept, ClusterJoin, ClusterSync, ShutdownTimeout, ShutdownFlush, and HealthEndpoint, each with message: String.

acceptance.criterion: ServerError derives Debug and uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)]
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:2 has #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)].

acceptance.criterion: ServerError is re-exported from lib.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/lib.rs:7 has pub use error::ServerError.

acceptance.criterion: No catch-all or Other variant exists
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:3-39 contains exactly the nine named variants and no Other, Unknown, or catch-all variant.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement configuration loading or parsing -- that is SRV-002.
SHALL NOT implement network listening or connection handling -- that is SRV-003.
SHALL NOT implement graceful shutdown logic -- that is SRV-004.
SHALL NOT implement clustering -- that is SRV-005.
SHALL NOT implement health endpoints -- that is SRV-006.
SHALL NOT add any messaging semantics -- the server delegates all publish, subscribe, and conversation logic to the liminal library crate (CN5).
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal-server -- -D warnings passes clean.
lib.rs contains only pub mod and pub use -- no logic.
Binary compiles to a single executable via cargo build -p liminal-server.
